body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: intent extractor learning substrate
body.intention: I intend that intent extractor consequences are observed — specifically whether learning improves predictions and whether the cc_queue.py add pipeline remains reliable.
body.description:
**What changed:** Intent extractor device built as canonical learning substrate; cc_queue.py add stamps inferred intentions (fail-open); Librarian research completions send validate() signals.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- predict() adds latency to cc_queue.py add even with fail-open — measure add time before/after
- Backfill validate() on closed tickets may generate low-quality signals if result fields are sparse
- patterns() may surface noise patterns before enough validations accumulate — early callers may get low-confidence garbage
- Learning delta test (baseline vs after-20) may show zero improvement if Haiku is already at ceiling on intent-from-prose — the few-shot mechanism would be complexity without payoff
- devlab.validations grows unboundedly — needs pruning/archival strategy eventually

**Signals to watch:** cc_queue.py add latency; devlab.validations row count at 2-week mark; learning delta measured during T-intent-extractor-device close; patterns() quality spot-check by Akien at 2 weeks.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-28 or when T-intent-extractor-backfill closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
